text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Done is better than perfect.
author: Sheryl Sandberg

text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Creativity is intelligence having fun.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
author: Anne Lamott

text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
author: Confucius

text: Focus on being productive instead of busy.
author: Tim Ferriss

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The chief enemy of creativity is good sense.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Amateurs sit and wait for inspiration, the rest of us just get up and go to work.
author: Stephen King

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Every child is an artist. The problem is how to remain an artist once we grow up.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: Until we can manage time, we can manage nothing else.
author: Peter Drucker

text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
author: Aristotle

text: Imagination is the beginning of creation.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: Be where you are, not where you think you should be.
author: Unknown

text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
author: Walt Disney

text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: You can't use up creativity. The more you use, the more you have.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Wherever you are, be there totally.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving your goals.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: If you spend too much time thinking about a thing, you'll never get it done.
author: Bruce Lee

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Art is not what you see, but what you make others see.
author: Edgar Degas

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
author: Sam Levenson

text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: We do not see things as they are, we see them as we are.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: The desire to create is one of the deepest yearnings of the human soul.
author: Dieter F. Uchtdorf

text: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.
author: Buddha

text: Everything you've ever wanted is on the other side of fear.
author: George Addair

text: It's not that I'm so smart, it's just that I stay with problems longer.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The only thing I know is that I know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: To be creative means to be in love with life.
author: Osho

text: Do every act of your life as though it were the very last act of your life.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Your limitation—it's only your imagination.
author: Unknown

text: Either you run the day or the day runs you.
author: Jim Rohn

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Creativity takes courage.
author: Henri Matisse

text: The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
author: William James

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: The key is not to prioritize what's on your schedule, but to schedule your priorities.
author: Stephen Covey

text: You cannot find peace by avoiding life.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: Learn the rules like a pro, so you can break them like an artist.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: In today's rush, we all think too much, seek too much, want too much, and forget about the joy of just being.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
author: Thomas Edison

text: Efficiency is doing things right; effectiveness is doing the right things.
author: Peter Drucker

text: The mind that opens to a new idea never returns to its original size.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The world always seems brighter when you've just made something that wasn't there before.
author: Neil Gaiman

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Hardships often prepare ordinary people for an extraordinary destiny.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Action is the foundational key to all success.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: A life spent making mistakes is not only more honorable, but more useful than a life spent doing nothing.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: Creativity is contagious, pass it on.
author: Albert Einstein

text: When you realize nothing is lacking, the whole world belongs to you.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Don't be pushed around by the fears in your mind. Be led by the dreams in your heart.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: Time is what we want most, but what we use worst.
author: William Penn

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Have no fear of perfection — you'll never reach it.
author: Salvador Dalí

text: Be happy in the moment, that's enough. Each moment is all we need, not more.
author: Mother Teresa

text: Whether you think you can or you think you can't, you're right.
author: Henry Ford

text: Plans are nothing; planning is everything.
author: Dwight D. Eisenhower

text: What we think, we become.
author: Buddha

text: The true sign of intelligence is not knowledge but imagination.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Breathe. Let go. And remind yourself that this very moment is the only one you know you have for sure.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: The harder you work for something, the greater you'll feel when you achieve it.
author: Unknown

text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
author: Arthur Ashe

text: In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on.
author: Robert Frost

text: Creativity is allowing yourself to make mistakes. Art is knowing which ones to keep.
author: Scott Adams

text: The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.
author: Rumi

text: If you want to achieve greatness stop asking for permission.
author: Unknown

text: A year from now you may wish you had started today.
author: Karen Lamb

text: Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: Every artist was first an amateur.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Smile, breathe, and go slowly.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Go the extra mile. It's never crowded there.
author: Dr. Wayne D. Dyer

text: Do the hard jobs first. The easy jobs will take care of themselves.
author: Dale Carnegie

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Curiosity about life in all of its aspects, I think, is still the secret of great creative people.
author: Leo Burnett

text: With the new day comes new strength and new thoughts.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Dream big and dare to fail.
author: Norman Vaughan

text: You don't need more time, you need fewer distractions.
author: Unknown

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama

text: The creative adult is the child who survived.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: Life is available only in the present moment.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Nothing is less productive than to make more efficient what should not be done at all.
author: Peter Drucker

text: An unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Within you, there is a stillness and a sanctuary to which you can retreat at any time.
author: Hermann Hesse

text: Small daily improvements over time lead to stunning results.
author: Robin Sharma

text: Productivity is never an accident. It is always the result of a commitment to excellence.
author: Paul J. Meyer

text: The only real failure is the failure to try.
author: Unknown

text: To live a creative life, we must lose our fear of being wrong.
author: Joseph Chilton Pearce

text: The best way to capture moments is to pay attention. This is how we cultivate mindfulness.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

text: The only people who never fail are those who never try.
author: Ilka Chase

text: Failure is just another way to learn how to do something right.
author: Marian Wright Edelman

text: I failed my way to success.
author: Thomas Edison

text: Every failure brings with it the seed of an equivalent success.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: Only those who dare to fail greatly can ever achieve greatly.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: It is hard to fail, but it is worse never to have tried to succeed.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: Hold fast to dreams, for if dreams die, life is a broken winged bird that cannot fly.
author: Langston Hughes

text: Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: We can do anything we want to do if we stick to it long enough.
author: Helen Keller

text: The more difficulties one has to encounter, the more significant and the higher in inspiration his life will be.
author: Horace Bushnell

text: I was always looking outside myself for strength and confidence, but it comes from within. It is there all the time.
author: Anna Freud

text: The difference between a successful person and others is not a lack of strength, not a lack of knowledge, but rather a lack in will.
author: Vince Lombardi

text: The best way out is always through.
author: Robert Frost

text: You miss 100 percent of the shots you don't take.
author: Wayne Gretzky

text: Nothing will ever be attempted if all possible objections must first be overcome.
author: Samuel Johnson

text: Success is the child of audacity.
author: Benjamin Disraeli

text: The only place where success comes before work is in the dictionary.
author: Vidal Sassoon

text: Being realistic is the most commonly traveled road to mediocrity.
author: Will Smith

text: The road to success is always under construction.
author: Arnold Palmer

text: Failure defeats losers, failure inspires winners.
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki

text: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for.
author: John A. Shedd

text: Victory comes only after many struggles and countless defeats.
author: Og Mandino

text: Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Build your own dreams, or someone else will hire you to build theirs.
author: Farrah Gray

text: You may be disappointed if you fail, but you are doomed if you don't try.
author: Beverly Sills

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow will be our doubts of today.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: It is never too late to be what you might have been.
author: George Eliot

text: There is only one way to avoid criticism: do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing.
author: Aristotle

text: When you have a dream, you've got to grab it and never let go.
author: Carol Burnett

text: When everything seems to be going against you, remember that the airplane takes off against the wind, not with it.
author: Henry Ford

text: There's no shortage of remarkable ideas, what's missing is the will to execute them.
author: Seth Godin

text: A successful man is one who can lay a firm foundation with the bricks others have thrown at him.
author: David Brinkley

text: Fall seven times, stand up eight.
author: Japanese Proverb

text: Most great people have attained their greatest success just one step beyond their greatest failure.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: If you cannot do great things, do small things in a great way.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: The successful warrior is the average man, with laser-like focus.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Risk more than others think is safe. Dream more than others think is practical.
author: Howard Schultz

text: I am not a product of my circumstances. I am a product of my decisions.
author: Stephen Covey

text: Nothing will work unless you do.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Do or do not. There is no try.
author: Yoda

text: What is not started will never get finished.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: Our deepest fear is not that we are inadequate. Our deepest fear is that we are powerful beyond measure.
author: Marianne Williamson

text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
author: William James

text: You are never too old to set another goal or to dream a new dream.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Courage is not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: If you hear a voice within you say you cannot paint, then by all means paint, and that voice will be silenced.
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: Perseverance is not a long race; it is many short races one after the other.
author: Walter Elliot

text: The only impossible journey is the one you never begin.
author: Tony Robbins

text: A diamond is a chunk of coal that did well under pressure.
author: Henry Kissinger

text: Keep your face always toward the sunshine, and shadows will fall behind you.
author: Walt Whitman

text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
author: Confucius

text: Commitment leads to action. Action brings your dream closer.
author: Marcia Wieder

text: Take time to deliberate; but when the time for action arrives, stop thinking and go in.
author: Andrew Jackson

text: Arriving at one goal is the starting point to another.
author: John Dewey

text: A goal is a dream with a deadline.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: Goals are the fuel in the furnace of achievement.
author: Brian Tracy

text: Take risks and you'll get the payoffs. Learn from your mistakes until you succeed.
author: Bobby Flay

text: Do not wait to strike until the iron is hot; but make it hot by striking.
author: William B. Sprague

text: Yesterday's home runs don't win today's games.
author: Babe Ruth

text: Always deliver more than expected.
author: Larry Page

text: Fail often so you can succeed sooner.
author: Tom Kelley

text: He who begins many things finishes but few.
author: German Proverb

text: Failing to plan is planning to fail.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Ideas are easy. Implementation is hard.
author: Guy Kawasaki

text: Don't worry about failure; you only have to be right once.
author: Drew Houston

text: Hire character. Train skill.
author: Peter Schultz

text: Our business in life is not to get ahead of others, but to get ahead of ourselves.
author: E. Joseph Cossman

text: You must either modify your dreams or magnify your skills.
author: Jim Rohn

text: Whenever you are asked if you can do a job, tell them, certainly I can! Then get busy and find out how to do it.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: The beginning is the most important part of the work.
author: Plato

text: Well begun is half done.
author: Aristotle

text: You don't have to see the whole staircase, just take the first step.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: If you want to make an easy job seem mighty hard, just keep putting off doing it.
author: Olin Miller

text: Concentrate all your thoughts upon the work at hand. The sun's rays do not burn until brought to a focus.
author: Alexander Graham Bell

text: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
author: Jim Rohn

text: An ounce of action is worth a ton of theory.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Great acts are made up of small deeds.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Work expands so as to fill the time available for its completion.
author: C. Northcote Parkinson

text: Don't confuse activity with productivity. Many people are simply busy being busy.
author: Robin Sharma

text: If you want something done, ask a busy person to do it.
author: Lucille Ball

text: The secret of getting things done is to act.
author: Dante Alighieri

text: Motivation is what gets you started. Habit is what keeps you going.
author: Jim Rohn

text: Do first things first, and second things not at all.
author: Peter Drucker

text: What gets measured gets managed.
author: Peter Drucker

text: It is not enough to be busy. The question is: what are we busy about?
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: By failing to prepare, you are preparing to fail.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Lost time is never found again.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: If you love life, don't waste time, for time is what life is made up of.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Don't count the days, make the days count.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: Absorb what is useful, discard what is not, add what is uniquely your own.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Vision without execution is hallucination.
author: Thomas Edison

text: The least productive people are usually the ones who are most in favor of holding meetings.
author: Thomas Sowell

text: Perfection is not attainable, but if we chase perfection we can catch excellence.
author: Vince Lombardi

text: Success is the sum of small efforts repeated day in and day out.
author: Robert Collier

text: Ordinary people think merely of spending time. Great people think of using it.
author: Arthur Schopenhauer

text: Have a bias toward action — let's see something happen now.
author: Lee Iacocca

text: Don't waste your life in doubts and fears; spend yourself on the work before you.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Do not anticipate trouble or worry about what may never happen. Keep in the sunlight.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: The mind that is anxious about future events is miserable.
author: Seneca

text: Years teach us more than books.
author: Berthold Auerbach

text: The only medicine for suffering, crime, and all the other woes of mankind, is wisdom.
author: Thomas Huxley

text: The art of being wise is knowing what to overlook.
author: William James

text: No man was ever wise by chance.
author: Seneca

text: The virtue lies in the struggle, not in the prize.
author: Richard Monckton Milnes

text: The whole problem with the world is that fools and fanatics are always so certain of themselves, but wiser people so full of doubts.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: If one does not know to which port one is sailing, no wind is favorable.
author: Seneca

text: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn't do than by the ones you did do.
author: Mark Twain

text: How you climb a mountain is more important than reaching the top.
author: Yvon Chouinard

text: It takes 20 years to build a reputation and five minutes to ruin it.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Everyone is a genius. But if you judge a fish by its ability to climb a tree, it will spend its whole life believing it is stupid.
author: Albert Einstein

text: A man must be big enough to admit his mistakes, smart enough to profit from them, and strong enough to correct them.
author: John C. Maxwell

text: A wise man learns by the mistakes of others, a fool by his own.
author: Latin Proverb

text: The diamond cannot be polished without friction, nor the man perfected without trials.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Vision without action is a daydream. Action without vision is a nightmare.
author: Japanese Proverb

text: Sow a thought, reap an action; sow an action, reap a habit; sow a habit, reap a character; sow a character, reap a destiny.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: It's fine to celebrate success but it is more important to heed the lessons of failure.
author: Bill Gates

text: Success is a lousy teacher. It seduces smart people into thinking they can't lose.
author: Bill Gates

text: The best use of life is to spend it for something that outlasts it.
author: William James

text: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
author: Aristotle

text: Wise men speak because they have something to say; fools because they have to say something.
author: Plato

text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Judge a man by his questions rather than his answers.
author: Voltaire

text: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: To know what you know and what you do not know, that is true knowledge.
author: Confucius

text: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
author: Plato

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: No one can make you feel inferior without your consent.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Experience is not what happens to you; it is what you do with what happens to you.
author: Aldous Huxley

text: The obstacle is the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: What you do speaks so loudly that I cannot hear what you say.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

text: The fool doth think he is wise, but the wise man knows himself to be a fool.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Time you enjoy wasting is not wasted time.
author: Marthe Troly-Curtin

text: The mind is not a vessel to be filled, but a fire to be kindled.
author: Plutarch

text: He who knows others is wise; he who knows himself is enlightened.
author: Lao Tzu

text: The pen is mightier than the sword.
author: Edward Bulwer-Lytton

text: By three methods we may learn wisdom: by reflection, which is noblest; by imitation, which is easiest; and by experience, which is the bitterest.
author: Confucius

text: When the student is ready, the teacher will appear.
author: Lao Tzu

text: The first step to wisdom is silence; the second is listening.
author: Hebrew Proverb

text: A person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Keep away from people who try to belittle your ambitions. Small people always do that, but the really great make you feel that you too can become great.
author: Mark Twain

text: You cannot depend on your eyes when your imagination is out of focus.
author: Mark Twain

text: Who looks outside, dreams. Who looks inside, awakes.
author: Carl Jung

text: Insist on yourself. Never imitate.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: If you're not embarrassed by the first version of your product, you've launched too late.
author: Reid Hoffman

text: If you want to be inventive, you have to be willing to fail.
author: Jeff Bezos

text: You don't learn to walk by following rules. You learn by doing and falling over.
author: Richard Branson

text: I don't have big ideas. I sometimes have small ideas, which seem to work out.
author: Matt Mullenweg

text: Creativity is seeing what others see and thinking what no one else ever thought.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The creative process is a process of surrender, not control.
author: Julia Cameron

text: Innovation distinguishes between a leader and a follower.
author: Steve Jobs

text: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
author: Peter Drucker

text: Logic will get you from A to B. Imagination will take you everywhere.
author: Albert Einstein

text: If I had asked people what they wanted, they would have said faster horses.
author: Henry Ford

text: Do not fear to be eccentric in opinion, for every opinion now accepted was once eccentric.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: An idea that is not dangerous is unworthy of being called an idea at all.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: There is no innovation and creativity without failure.
author: Brené Brown

text: Originality is nothing but judicious imitation.
author: Voltaire

text: The secret to creativity is knowing how to hide your sources.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Creativity requires the courage to let go of certainties.
author: Erich Fromm

text: Everything you can imagine is real.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Others have seen what is and asked why. I have seen what could be and asked why not.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Make visible what, without you, might perhaps never have been seen.
author: Robert Bresson

text: The painter has the universe in his mind and hands.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Around here, we don't look backwards for very long. We keep moving forward, opening up new doors and doing new things.
author: Walt Disney

text: The creation of something new is not accomplished by the intellect but by the play instinct.
author: Carl Jung

text: Great things are done by a series of small things brought together.
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: I begin with an idea and then it becomes something else.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: The earth has music for those who listen.
author: William Shakespeare

text: One must still have chaos in oneself to be able to give birth to a dancing star.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: In order to be irreplaceable one must always be different.
author: Coco Chanel

text: Necessity is the mother of invention.
author: Plato

text: Clean out a corner of your mind and creativity will instantly fill it.
author: Dee Hock

text: The only unique contribution that we will ever make in this world will be born of our creativity.
author: Brené Brown

text: A creative life is an amplified life. It's a bigger life, a happier life, an expanded life, and a hell of a lot more interesting life.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: You can't wait for inspiration. You have to go after it with a club.
author: Jack London

text: The works must be conceived with fire in the soul but executed with clinical coolness.
author: Joan Miró

text: Art is the lie that enables us to realize the truth.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Creativity is just connecting things.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Imagination is the only weapon in the war against reality.
author: Lewis Carroll

text: The position of the artist is humble. He is essentially a channel.
author: Piet Mondrian

text: If you can dream it, you can do it.
author: Walt Disney

text: Don't think. Thinking is the enemy of creativity.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: One of the huge mistakes people make is that they try to force an interest on themselves. You don't choose your passions; your passions choose you.
author: Jeff Bezos

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is not easy to find happiness in ourselves, and it is not possible to find it elsewhere.
author: Agnes Repplier

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Happiness is not a goal, but a by-product.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Happiness is not a state to arrive at, but a manner of traveling.
author: Margaret Lee Runbeck

text: Action may not always bring happiness; but there is no happiness without action.
author: Benjamin Disraeli

text: Happiness depends more on the inward disposition of mind than on outward circumstances.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: I avoid looking forward or backward, and try to keep looking upward.
author: Charlotte Brontë

text: The great lesson is that the sacred is in the ordinary, that it is to be found in one's daily life.
author: Abraham Maslow

text: Life can be pulled by goals just as surely as it can be pushed by drives.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: In between goals is a thing called life, that has to be lived and enjoyed.
author: Sid Caesar

text: Everyone should carefully observe which way his heart draws him, and then choose that way with all his strength.
author: Hasidic Proverb

text: Teachers open the door. You enter by yourself.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: I learned not to worry so much about the outcome, but to concentrate on the step I was on.
author: Steve Wozniak

text: Remembering you are going to die is the best way I know to avoid the trap of thinking you have something to lose.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Live in each season as it passes; breathe the air, drink the drink, taste the fruit.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: There is nothing either good or bad, but thinking makes it so.
author: William Shakespeare

text: He who is not contented with what he has, would not be contented with what he would like to have.
author: Socrates

text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: Yesterday is history, tomorrow is a mystery, today is a gift of God, which is why we call it the present.
author: Bill Keane

text: Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: You cannot always control what goes on outside. But you can always control what goes on inside.
author: Wayne Dyer

text: The soul always knows what to do to heal itself. The challenge is to silence the mind.
author: Caroline Myss

text: Be content with what you have; rejoice in the way things are. When you realize there is nothing lacking, the whole world belongs to you.
author: Lao Tzu

text: The things that matter most must never be at the mercy of the things that matter least.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: If you want to conquer the anxiety of life, live in the moment, live in the breath.
author: Amit Ray

text: Knowing how to be solitary is central to the art of loving.
author: bell hooks

text: To a mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Respond; don't react. Listen; don't talk. Think; don't assume.
author: Raji Lukkoor

text: The only way to live is by accepting each minute as an unrepeatable miracle.
author: Tara Brach

text: Happiness is the absence of the striving for happiness.
author: Zhuangzi

text: Let go of the thoughts that don't make you strong.
author: Karen Salmansohn

text: What we achieve inwardly will change outer reality.
author: Plutarch

text: Tension is who you think you should be. Relaxation is who you are.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: If you are depressed you are living in the past. If you are anxious you are living in the future. If you are at peace you are living in the present.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you ever have.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Everything that has a beginning has an ending. Make your peace with that and all will be well.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: In the end, just three things matter: how well we have lived, how well we have loved, how well we have learned to let go.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: Rule your mind or it will rule you.
author: Horace

text: Make happy those who are near, and those who are far will come.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Your calm mind is the ultimate weapon against your challenges.
author: Bryant McGill

text: Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.
author: Buddha

text: The secret of change is to focus all of your energy not on fighting the old, but on building the new.
author: Socrates

text: Strive not to be a success, but rather to be of value.
author: Albert Einstein

text: One percent better every day.
author: James Clear

text: Never mistake motion for action.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: People often say that motivation doesn't last. Well, neither does bathing. That's why we recommend it daily.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: To think is easy. To act is hard. But the hardest thing in the world is to act in accordance with your thinking.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: Someday is not a day of the week.
author: Janet Dailey

text: Without hustle, talent will only carry you so far.
author: Gary Vaynerchuk

text: The two most important days in your life are the day you are born and the day you find out why.
author: Mark Twain

text: All that we are is the result of what we have thought.
author: Buddha

text: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The only constant in life is change.
author: Heraclitus

text: I think, therefore I am.
author: René Descartes

text: To thine own self be true.
author: William Shakespeare

text: To create, one must first question everything.
author: Eileen Gray

text: Creativity is the power to connect the seemingly unconnected.
author: William Plomer

text: A truly creative person rids him or herself of all self-imposed limitations.
author: Gerald Jampolsky

text: Without freedom, there is no creation.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Rules are what the artist breaks; the memorable never emerged from a formula.
author: Bill Bernbach

text: The artist is not a special kind of person; rather each person is a special kind of artist.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: Vulnerability is the birthplace of innovation, creativity, and change.
author: Brené Brown

text: You see things and you say why. But I dream things that never were and I say why not.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: We are shaped by our thoughts; we become what we think.
author: Buddha

text: Walk as if you are kissing the earth with your feet.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Few of us ever live in the present. We are forever anticipating what is to come or remembering what has gone.
author: Louis L'Amour

text: Nothing ever goes away until it has taught us what we need to know.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: Calmness of mind is one of the beautiful jewels of wisdom.
author: James Allen

text: Look past your thoughts, so you may drink the pure nectar of this moment.
author: Rumi

text: One conscious breath in and out is a meditation.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: You only lose what you cling to.
author: Buddha

text: Surrender to what is. Let go of what was. Have faith in what will be.
author: Sonia Ricotti

text: Each morning we are born again. What we do today is what matters most.
author: Buddha

text: Stop, look, and listen to your heart, hear what it's telling you.
author: Diana Ross

text: Great things never come from comfort zones.
author: Ben Francia

text: Knowledge speaks, but wisdom listens.
author: Jimi Hendrix

text: The best things in life aren't things.
author: Art Buchwald

text: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one's courage.
author: Anaïs Nin
